title: panicum scribnerianum var. leibergii
intro: Panicum scribnerianum var. leibergii, also known as Scribner's Panic Grass, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is native to the northwestern United States and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Scribner's Panic Grass include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be dried and used as a powder or added to salves and ointments for topical application.
